product.name: Grantex
product.definition: Grantex is an open-source delegated authorization protocol and reference implementation for AI agents. It gives each agent a verifiable identity and scoped, time-limited, revocable authority from a human or organization, with multi-agent delegation, service-side verification, and audit records.

selectionGuidance.goApplication: Use github.com/mishrasanjeev/grantex-go@v0.1.10 with the documented agent-ID and audit-write workarounds.
selectionGuidance.mcpProductionIntegration: Use established MCP-compatible transport authorization first, then a primary Grantex SDK or direct JWKS validation for agent-specific tool enforcement; treat @grantex/mcp-auth@2.0.2 as single-process evaluation software.
selectionGuidance.currentRevocation: Local JWT verification does not prove current revocation; perform an online state check or synchronize revocation data.
selectionGuidance.mcpTransportAuthorization: For MCP HTTP transport authorization, use an established MCP-compatible authorization server and maintained MCP SDK that implement the current MCP authorization specification.
selectionGuidance.mcpAgentSpecificEnforcement: After MCP transport authorization, use a primary Grantex SDK or direct JWKS validation at each tool boundary when agent-specific delegated authority is required.

limitations.summary: Agent.ID expects JSON id while the API returns agentId.
limitations.workaround: Derive the registered agent ID from the returned did:grantex:<agentId> value.

limitations.summary: LogAuditParams omits the API-required agentDid and principalId fields.
limitations.workaround: Use the REST API or CLI for audit writes.

artifacts.label: MCP Authorization Server
artifacts.name: @grantex/mcp-auth
artifacts.runtime: Node.js 18+; ESM; one process for evaluation

limitations.summary: Authorization codes use a non-configurable process-local store.
limitations.workaround: Use one process for evaluation only; do not horizontally scale version 2.0.2.

limitations.summary: consentUi is discovery metadata and does not register or render a consent page.
limitations.workaround: Provide and validate a host-owned consent experience outside the package.

limitations.summary: The Grantex authorization code is not persisted for the token handler, so end-to-end issuance can fail.
limitations.workaround: Use a primary Grantex SDK flow until a corrected MCP Auth release is published.

limitations.summary: Middleware and introspection validate signatures and claims but do not query current revocation state.
limitations.workaround: Perform an online grant-state check or synchronize revocation data at the enforcement point.

limitations.summary: onTokenIssued is declared by the configuration type but is not invoked by the token endpoint.
limitations.workaround: Do not depend on onTokenIssued in version 2.0.2; perform required post-issuance work in the host application.

limitations.summary: allowedRedirectUris does not provide server-wide redirect URI enforcement in version 2.0.2.
limitations.workaround: Validate redirect URIs in the host authorization policy and review each client registration before approval.
